period_tips_001_desc: Drinking enough water reduces cramps and keeps your energy up during your period.
period_tips_002_desc: Your body loses iron during menstruation. Include spinach, lentils and nuts daily.
period_tips_003_desc: Gentle walks or yoga can ease bloating and improve your mood during your cycle.
period_tips_004_desc: Applying heat to your lower abdomen relaxes muscles and eases menstrual cramps effectively.
period_tips_005_desc: Getting 7-8 hours of sleep helps regulate hormones and reduces period fatigue.
period_tips_006_desc: Noting your symptoms daily helps you predict patterns and manage your cycle better.
period_tips_007_desc: Chamomile and ginger tea can soothe cramps and reduce bloating naturally.
period_awareness_001_desc: Knowing your cycle length helps you predict your next period accurately.
period_awareness_002_desc: PMS symptoms appear 1-2 weeks before your period. Mood changes, bloating and fatigue are common.
period_awareness_003_desc: Flow can be light, medium or heavy. Knowing your typical flow helps you choose the right products.
period_awareness_004_desc: Estrogen and progesterone fluctuate throughout your cycle affecting mood and energy levels.
period_awareness_005_desc: Extremely heavy flow, severe pain or irregular cycles lasting months may need medical attention.
period_avoid_001_desc: Too much coffee or tea can worsen cramps and increase anxiety during your period.
period_avoid_002_desc: Excess salt causes water retention and bloating which worsens period discomfort.
period_avoid_003_desc: Skipping meals causes blood sugar drops which can worsen mood swings and fatigue.
period_avoid_004_desc: High sugar intake causes energy crashes and can intensify cramps during menstruation.
period_avoid_005_desc: Tight clothes around the waist can increase pressure and discomfort during your period.
pregnancy_tips_001_desc: Folic acid is critical in the first 12 weeks. Include leafy greens and fortified cereals.
pregnancy_tips_002_desc: Light walking for 30 minutes daily improves circulation and reduces pregnancy fatigue.
pregnancy_tips_003_desc: Take prenatal vitamins with iron and DHA daily as recommended by your healthcare provider.
pregnancy_tips_004_desc: Eat small frequent meals and keep crackers nearby. Ginger tea can help reduce nausea.
pregnancy_tips_005_desc: Drink at least 8-10 glasses of water daily to support increased blood volume during pregnancy.
pregnancy_tips_006_desc: Kegel exercises strengthen pelvic muscles and help prepare your body for delivery.
pregnancy_awareness_001_desc: Pregnancy has three trimesters of roughly 13 weeks each with different development milestones.
pregnancy_awareness_002_desc: Major organs form in the first trimester. By week 20 you can usually feel baby movements.
pregnancy_awareness_003_desc: Regular checkups include blood tests, ultrasounds and glucose screening at different stages.
pregnancy_awareness_004_desc: Weight gain, skin changes and joint loosening are normal adaptations during pregnancy.
pregnancy_avoid_001_desc: Raw fish, unpasteurised dairy and undercooked meat carry bacteria harmful to your baby.
pregnancy_avoid_002_desc: Both smoking and alcohol can cause serious developmental issues and complications for your baby.
pregnancy_avoid_003_desc: Keep caffeine under 200mg per day. Excessive caffeine is linked to low birth weight.
pregnancy_avoid_004_desc: Many over-the-counter drugs are unsafe during pregnancy. Always consult your doctor first.
